title: DNA extraction from whatman filter papers
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dc4us","text":"This protocols is for RNA extraction from Whatman filter paper.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Filter paper disc containing blood cut and placed into 1.5 mL EP tubes.
Centrifuge at 20,000 × g for 00:05:00 , discard the supernatant with a micropipettor. go to step #2 until the DNA precipitate forms a white or pink gel-like pellet at the bottom of the tube.
Measure absorbance at 230nm, 260nm, and 280nm, Calculate the A260/A280 and A230/A260 ratio.
Resuspend the pellet in 150 µL 10% Chelex-100 solution，vortex the sample briefly for 00:00:30 . Chelex 100 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C7901-100G
Add 1 mL RNase-free water and vortex to mix. DNAse/RNAse free distilled water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10977023
Incubate at 25 °C, Room temperature for 00:15:00 to lyse RBC.
Carefully transfer the supernatant to a clean 1.5 mL EP tube with a micropipettor. Proceed to downstream applications, or store the DNA at -20 °C .
creator: Xiang Guo
